segments.text: First listen, and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: For how long did the writer give up smoking?
After reading an article entitled, Cigarette Smoking and Your Health, I lit a cigarette to calm my nerves.

translations.zh-Hans: 读完一篇题为《吸烟与健康》的文章后，我点燃一支香烟来平复情绪。

segments.text: I smoked with concentration and pleasure as I was sure that this would be my last cigarette.

translations.zh-Hans: 我专注而惬意地吸着，因为我确信这会是自己的最后一支烟。

segments.text: For a whole week I did not smoke at all and during this time, my wife suffered terribly.

translations.zh-Hans: 整整一周我一支烟也没吸，而在这段时间里，我妻子吃尽了苦头。

segments.text: I had all the usual symptoms of someone giving up smoking.

translations.zh-Hans: 戒烟者常有的症状我全都有。

segments.text: A bad temper and an enormous appetite.
My friends kept on offering me cigarettes and cigars.

translations.zh-Hans: 朋友们不断递给我香烟和雪茄。

segments.text: They made no effort to hide their amusement whenever I produced a packet of sweets from my pocket.

translations.zh-Hans: 每当我从口袋里掏出一包糖果时，他们都毫不掩饰自己的好笑。

segments.text: After seven days of this, I went to a party.

translations.zh-Hans: 这样过了七天后，我参加了一个聚会。

segments.text: Everybody around me was smoking and I felt extremely uncomfortable.

translations.zh-Hans: 周围每个人都在吸烟，我感到极不自在。

segments.text: When my old friend Brian urged me to accept a cigarette, it was more than I could bear.

translations.zh-Hans: 老朋友布赖恩劝我接过一支烟时，我再也忍不住了。

segments.text: I took one guiltily, lit it and smoked with satisfaction.

translations.zh-Hans: 我内疚地拿了一支，点燃后心满意足地吸了起来。

segments.text: My wife was delighted that things had returned to normal once more.

translations.zh-Hans: 妻子很高兴，一切终于又恢复了正常。

segments.text: Anyway, as Brian pointed out, it is the easiest thing in the world to give up smoking.

translations.zh-Hans: 不过，正如布赖恩指出的那样，戒烟是世界上最容易的事情。

segments.text: He himself has done it lots of times.

translations.zh-Hans: 他自己都戒过很多次了。
